TEXT.English: [H1]K-Mod to AdvCiv Changes[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Provided by AdvCiv-SAS maintainer, and non-exhaustive list of some info i'd want to display there as well, from AdvCiv's manual.pdf or similar path for manual.txt or some other AdvCiv-related sources.[PARAGRAPH:1]Info below may also not be updated with latest version of the manual.pdf or similar format, view them rather at base AdvCiv's github https://github.com/f1rpo/AdvCiv or some other place that has them or is relevant for latest updated content or more exhaustive info, this below only provides some key or excerpted info for quick view.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Notable adjustments to K-Mod AI behavior (non-exhaustive as well):[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- AI razes fewer cities and less randomly; 116[NEWLINE]- uses Slavery and Drafting less aggressively, though still frequently; 121b, 017[NEWLINE]- uses destructive espionage missions only against civs it dislikes; 120[NEWLINE]- will rarely found a religion before a second city (reinforcing K-Mod changes that make the AI less interested in the early religions). 171[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Reverted by AdvCiv (i (AdvCiv-SAS maintainer/developer) assume from K-Mod (non-exhaustive as well)):[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- See Immortal Culture about reverted changes to culture and revolts.[NEWLINE]- Production overflow is never invested into an additional unit as this had lead to balance problems with AI civs training multiple Archers per turn. Instead, excess overflow is converted into gold as in the Unofficial Patch, though with some tweaks to make this conversion more difficult to abuse. 064b[NEWLINE]- Gifting Great People to the AI does not provide a relations bonus. 141[NEWLINE]- No impact of global research on inflation; instead, adjusted tech costs based on handicap and the years-per-turn progression for a more historically accurate tech pace, and Immortal and Deity games now start on turn 10. 251, 910[NEWLINE]- Some minor balance changes undone that weren't pulling their weight in terms of added complexity (change 200) (non-exhaustive (AdvCiv-SAS maintainer/develoepr note)): All corporations have the same maintenance multiplier (i.e. mostly as in BtS)[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Important miscellaneous changes in AdvCiv (non-exhaustive as well)[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- Workers and settlers only have a 50% chance of being captured and 0% if attacked on the same turn as declaring war. 010[NEWLINE]- When a tile with unfinished worker builds is left alone for more than 7 turns, the invested worker turns begin to decay. This is mainly to disincentivize pre-chopping. 011[NEWLINE]- Forest and Jungle provide only 25% defense, and none if the tile is owned by the attacker. 012[NEWLINE]- Unowned Forests and Jungles can't be chopped down. 119[NEWLINE]- The AI evacuates land units that can't defend well from cities that are about to be attacked and untenable. 139[NEWLINE]- When the AI has more happiness or health than it needs, it may refuse to accept resources in trade or may trade away its only copy of a resource. 036[NEWLINE]- Undefended cities stop "fearing for their safety" once Nationalism is discovered. 500c[NEWLINE]- Slavery base yield reduced to 24 production (from 30). 912d [NEWLINE]- The Spy unit can investigate rival cities, but (unlike in Warlords) with a mission cost. 103[NEWLINE]- Research progress toward a tech makes that tech cheaper to steal. 120i[NEWLINE]- When a citizen starves, the Granary is emptied to delay further starvation. 160[NEWLINE]- The Apostolic Palace can propose war against (non-full) voting members, but only full members are compelled to declare war. kekm.25[NEWLINE]- Can no longer bypass the Theocracy restriction by gifting missionaries. 123a/ kekm.4[NEWLINE]- Damage from nuclear weapons to units made less predictable; SDI interception chance reduced from 75% to 60% and cost increased from 1000 to 1500 production. 650[NEWLINE]- Wonders that have been manually removed from the production queue generate no "fail gold." Also no gold from national units. 123f[NEWLINE]- Trade routes can only come from revealed cities, and the paths to those cities need to be revealed as well. Cities are temporarily unavailable for trade when in disorder. Trade along rivers doesn't require any tech (as in Warlords; BtS demands Sailing). When borders of a war enemy block trade, a naval blockade can override this. Connecting a resource on an island workable by a mainland city requires only a road, not a fort. 124[NEWLINE]- When mountain peaks block off part of a landmass, the parts are treated as separate continents. This is relevant for the placement of Barbarians, colony maintenance and wonders that affect only one continent - though the main point is to make things easier for the AI. 030[NEWLINE]- Land threshold for Domination victory reduced, especially in large games. 254[NEWLINE]- On Emperor handicap and above, some of the AI discounts have been reduced, but human civs face increased production costs, research costs, city growth thresholds and Great Person thresholds. The AI also starts with fewer freebies, in particular, without Agriculture on Immortal and Deity and without a second free Settler on Deity. Progressive AI bonuses are now based on the game turn instead of the AI era. 251/ 250e[NEWLINE]- Marathon speed is only 2.5 times slower than Normal speed, not 3 times. 252[NEWLINE]- The default player count is 8 for the Standard map size. 137[NEWLINE]- Map generation: Clusters of Gold, Gems made less common. Silver may occur on Grassland Forest. No Flood Plains on river corners. Jungle may occur on Plains. 129[NEWLINE]- New algorithm for assigning starting locations. Should partition the available land more evenly than in BtS. Not enabled for all map scripts (list). 027, 108[NEWLINE]- Switched the no-war-probability of Roosevelt and Darius, meaning that Darius can start wars when pleased, and Roosevelt - normally - cannot. 005a[NEWLINE]- Suryavarman's favorite religion is Hinduism and his favorite civic Caste System. 005a[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Other information i scraped/found (long ago (relatively) (at the start of this mod) and can finally add in AdvCiv-SAS):[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:2]About the below info in this section, it comes from/in code comments from base AdvCiv or similar that i have removed our mod (mostly from the old strategy history panel fields we don't use anymore as they are/can be inaccurate/hard to update as well) (non-exhaustive as well); note that these below are not from base AdvCiv's manual so they may be inaccurate even more or not than manual doc added in this page (Mods Info/ Changes from K-Mod to AdvCiv); please also note that we don't update the [LINK=CONCEPT_ADVANCED_START]concepts pages (now in "Outdated" category in AdvCiv-SAS's sevopedia)[\LINK] so part of the info below (at least the one related to concepts, may not apply anymore to our mod at all and is just base AdvCiv info for exhaustiveness, as for all other info we altered some changes made by AdvCiv while we kept many or some, please view docs)[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]- (AdvCiv): Defense from Walls is not cumulative with defense from city culture. The higher of the two values counts.[NEWLINE]- (AdvCiv): The defense modifier from Castle replaces the one from Wall and is not cumulative with defense from city culture[NEWLINE]- units in friendly cities and forts are safe from Flank Attacks.[NEWLINE]- SISTINE_CHAPEL (note (in) advciv-sas: in AdvCiv-SAS is now replaced with [LINK=BUILDING_NATYA_SHASTRA]Natya Shastra[\LINK] and this is fixed too i.e. it now doubles after x turns like all other buildings too) unlike other building [ICON_CULTURE] rates, the added [ICON_CULTURE] rate doesn't double after 1000 years.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_ESPIONAGE Addition: advc.132 allows switching to civics not run by the Spy owner.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_CITIES advc.004f: "We Love the King Day" disabled by AdvCiv.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_LATE_START advc.250b: Added mention of the new SPaH option and a clarification that later-era start and advanced start are separate things. Overwrites text in Civ4GameText_BTS.xml.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_UNITS advc.010: Worker capturing has limitations; advc.005e.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_VASSAL_STATES advc.141: Pointed out change in happiness rule. Removed mention of vassal maintenance "dragging the economy to a halt"; it's not so dramatic. Vassal maint. is still mentioned.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_COMBAT and added info that feature defense depends on tile ownership.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_TRANSPORT Added mention of Executives as special cargo.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_TRANSPORT advc.123c: Rule change for loading units into cargo mentioned.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_DIPLOMACY Referring reader to the manual about AI refusal to talk (advc.104i) and tribute demands (advc.130o); clarified that the manner of declaring war doesn't make a difference, that tribute can't be demanded from pleased AI leaders, and that the AI doesn't normally declare war when the player declines an offer. Removed mention of "disband[ing] buildings" as this is not possible.[NEWLINE]- TXT_KEY_TRAIT_CREATIVE_PEDIA (note: advc.908b from my notes of these?) Since K-Mod and AdvCiv make [ICON_CULTURE] points more worthwhile in several ways, AdvCiv reduces the [ICON_CULTURE] rate (was 2 in BtS) and adds some free initial [ICON_CULTURE] instead. AdvCiv has also removed the faster [ICON_PRODUCTION] of Colosseum - which doesn't seem to fit the culture theme well. (note: advc.908b from my notes of these?) [NEWLINE]- _CIVIC_STATE_PROPERTY_ K-Mod treats colony maintenance as separate from distance maintenance, so this civic no longer affects colony maintenance.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_PROMOTIONS Overwrites CIV4GameText_Civilopedia_Concepts. Just correcting a bit of misinformation: When it comes to combat XP, it does matter who attacked.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_RESOURCES Overwrites CIV4GameText_Civilopedia_Concepts. Correction: Duplicate resources do have uses with BtS.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_COMBAT advc.012: Corrected two examples that say that Forest grants 50% def, Weirdly, an example in TXT_KEY_CONCEPT_TERRAIN_PEDIA already said that Forest defense is 25%. Perhaps it was changed to 50% late in development.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_ESPIONAGE [advc.103] Added note about Spies being able to investigate cities.[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_FOOD [advc.160] Replaced "half" of the food with "a portion". Added mention of the Granary under "Insufficient Food".[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_TRANSPORT advc.906: Submarine becomes Attack Submarine (note: i also added this note if i am mistaken and does not come from base AdvCiv, adding it for exhaustiveness and just in case as well......: advc.005e (but did not check 005e though))[NEWLINE]- CONCEPT_UNITED_NATIONS advc.148: Overwriting the key in CIV4GameText_Civilopedia_Concepts.xml. AI voting behavior outlined. Victory vote was missing entirely in German.[NEWLINE]- CIV_INCA advc.004g: Dropped mention of Quechua

[H1]Rivers[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Rivers are exceptional terrain in that they are not "in" or "on" a tile, but they run between tiles. A river is considered adjacent to all tiles that it runs along, including all four tiles which touch a place where the river bends or empties into water. However, the two tiles which touch the source of a river and are behind it, with respect to the river's flow, are not riverside. Rivers supply fresh water to all adjacent tiles. A river adds +1 [ICON_COMMERCE] to all adjacent land tiles, unless they also contain an unimproved forest or jungle. Only riverside Cities may build [LINK=BUILDING_LEVEE]Levees[\LINK], which provide +1 [ICON_PRODUCTION] to every riverside tile within that City's radius.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Terrain_(Civ4)#Rivers

[H1]Global Warming[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Global warming is a game element in Civilization IV: Beyond the Sword.[NEWLINE][H2]Overview[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]Global warming is caused by pollution (namely, "unhealthy" buildings) and nuclear explosions (which may sound counter-intuitive but is seemingly normal in the Civilization series). It is adverted by a large number of forests and jungles present on the world map. Its effects are to reduce the production of tiles, usually turning random tiles into desert. The tiles affected when global warming strikes are picked randomly, regardless of how much each nation contributed to its occurrence.[NEWLINE][H2]Mechanics[\H2][NEWLINE]Effect of Global Warming[PARAGRAPH:1]If Global Warming strikes, a random land PLOT which is not a city will be chosen.[PARAGRAPH:1]There is no regional, player, team or other bias in choosing the target plot for Global Warming. It does not matter who, where or why caused the nuclear explosions, built the polluting buildings, chopped or preserved the forests.[PARAGRAPH:1]If PLOT has a feature (Forest, Jungle, Floodplain, Oasis), this feature will be removed, but the terrain type does not change (unless the feature is Fallout, in which case nothing happens at all).[PARAGRAPH:1]If PLOT has no Feature and can give any yield (i.e. is not desert or frozen), then it will be turned into GLOBAL_WARMING_TERRAIN, which is - per standard XML - desert.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Note on probability[PARAGRAPH:1]This de facto reduces the Probability of Global Warming with increasing amount of useless land plots on the map. Since if a plot is chosen to be victim of Global Warming, that can not be worked anyway, nothing happens. So actually:[PARAGRAPH:1]PROBGW_strikes = 1 - (1 - PROB1 + PROB1*#BADLAND/#LAND)GW_VALUE[PARAGRAPH:1]Depending on how high PROB1 actually is and how much "bad land" we have it might result in 5-10 % (or even more in really extreme cases) less visible GW.[PARAGRAPH:1]Same thing with fallout. Should be negligible in most games, but could become interesting in a huge Nuclear War, where the Fallout will actually help preserve the terrain under it. Of course, as soon as you clear the Fallout after the war, the plots will become affected by Global Warming again.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Notes[PARAGRAPH:1]Nukes now count only half. This means that a single explosion (say a meltdown) will not cause GW by itself. However this is only true if there are no other sources of GW.[PARAGRAPH:1]The [ICON_UNHEALTHY] from buildings is weighted by map size (number of plots).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source with more details: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Global_warming_(Civ4)

[H1]Customization[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Civilization IV is much more open to modification than its predecessors. Game data and rules are stored in XML files, and a Software Development Kit was released in April 2006 to allow AI customization. Major parts of the interface, map generation, and scripted events are written entirely in Python and can be customised.[NEWLINE][H2]World Builder[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]The World Builder allows a player to create a map from scratch, to use an in-game situation as a starting point for a new scenario, or to simply cheat by giving himself more units, gold, buildings, better relations with leaders, improvements, cultural borders, technologies, even take over other cities by placing a border there. The terrain can be altered, and resources, military units and cities on the map can be added, removed or modified. Additionally, each civilization's technological progress as well as its diplomatic and military ties to other civilizations can be edited. The World Builder for Civ IV is in-game, in contrast to previous Civilization games where the Map Editor was an external application. (In Civ2 one could modify anything using the cheat menu.)[PARAGRAPH:1]World Builder can also be used to create an interesting experience. In custom game mode, the user must first turn all opposing civilizations off as well as all victory conditions. Barbarians must also be deactivated. In World Builder, the user can make it so that the player only starts with a settler with no starting technologies. This allows the user to progress through all technologies for a more expanded experience of the development of civilization.[NEWLINE][H2]XML[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]More game attributes are stored in XML files, which must be edited with an external text editor or application. Barry Caudill, a senior producer at Firaxis Games, said [1] in September:[PARAGRAPH:1]"Editing these files will allow players to tweak simple game rules and change or add content. For instance, they can add new unit or building types, change the cost of wonders, or add new civilizations. Players can also change the sounds played at certain times or edit the play list for your soundtrack."[PARAGRAPH:1]At the current time the XML processing in Windows is more permissive of minor errors than in Mac OS X. As a result, some XML files which will work on the Windows version of the game need some slight tweaking before they function on the Macintosh version.[NEWLINE][H2]Python[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]The game uses boost.python to allow the Python programming language access to many parts of the game (including the style and content of all interface screens). Python can also be used to modify random map generation and to add complex scripted events.[PARAGRAPH:1]The version of Python present in the Windows version of the game differs from the version in Mac OS X up to and including version 10.4.7, and as a result, while most Python files for the Windows version will work on the Macintosh version, not all will.[NEWLINE][H2]Software Development Kit[\H2][PARAGRAPH:1]The Civilization IV Software Development Kit was released on April 13 2006 to coincide with the release of the v1.61 patch. The kit allows players to view, modify, or completely re-write the game's DLL source code, enabling the modification of the game's AI and other integral parts of the game.[PARAGRAPH:1]As of the first official patch for the Macintosh version (v1.61 Revision A), there is no SDK for the Macintosh version of the game. In fact the Macintosh version lacks the separate library of game related code which the PC version uses, but instead includes the code compiled into the main executable. There is as of yet no indication of whether this will change in a future patch.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Civilization_IV#Customization

[H1]Fresh Water[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Any tile adjacent to a river, lake, or oasis has fresh water. The following rules apply to tiles with fresh water:[PARAGRAPH:1][ICON_BULLET]Any city built on a fresh water tile gains a permanent bonus of +2 [ICON_HEALTHY].[PARAGRAPH:1][ICON_BULLET][LINK=IMPROVEMENT_FARM]Farms[\LINK] require fresh water for [LINK=CONCEPT_IRRIGATION]irrigation[\LINK].[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Fresh_water_(Civ4)

[H1]Irrigation[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2][LINK=IMPROVEMENT_FARM]Farms[\LINK] require irrigation in order to provide their full [LINK=CONCEPT_FOOD]food[\LINK] benefits. Irrigation can be achieved by building the farm adjacent to a source of [LINK=CONCEPT_FRESH_WATER]fresh water[\LINK], like a river, lake, or oasis.[PARAGRAPH:2][LINK=TECH_CIVIL_SERVICE]Civil Service[\LINK] enables irrigated farms to become a source of irrigation themselves. That allows you to bring irrigation to distant locations by building a chain of farms from the source of fresh water.[PARAGRAPH:2]With the advent of [LINK=TECH_BIOLOGY]Biology[\LINK], Farms no longer require irrigation.

[H1]Scoring System[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]In Civilization IV the leadership skills of players are compared to a subjective list of twenty of the best or worst leaders in history, similar to the list in Civilization I. The score is based on a number of factors, including military growth and success, technological advancement, construction of wonders and economic growth. It is shown at the end of the game.[PARAGRAPH:2]The released version of the game abandoned Civilization III's graded scale. In Civ3, a spectacular victory on the easiest handicap would provide the player with only a middling score, and the best titles were only awarded to players attempting the hardest difficulties. The original Civ4, on the other hand, allows the player to obtain any score on any handicap level. As of the v1.61 patch, the grading system has returned to the curved-by-handicap scale.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Source with more info: https://civilization.fandom.com/wiki/Civilization_IV#Scoring_system
(K-Mod) Public Transportation and Recycling Center reduce pollution. Apart from avoiding unhealthiness in cities, this also slows down Global Warming.
The "Concepts (Outdated)" page in the Sevopedia summarizes the changes introduced by the "Beyond the Sword" expansion.

[H1]Score Tab Columns[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]This page explains each column shown in the Score tab table.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Leader (icon): leader portrait button.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Civ (icon): civilization icon button.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Col: player color marker.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]ID: player ID (sortable unique identifier).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Leader (text): leader/player name (or unknown if unmet).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]T1: primary leader trait icon.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]T2: secondary leader trait icon.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Att: attitude smiley toward selected perspective player.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Att#: numeric attitude total (+/-), attitude-colored.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Score: current score.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]dSc: score change over the last turn interval (+/-, colored).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]PowR: power ratio vs selected perspective player (scoreboard-style coloring).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]PowT: absolute total military power value for that player.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Cit: city count (revealed count unless debug).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]PoT/C: per-city military power, computed as PowT / Cit for all rows.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Land%: percentage of world land tiles (2 decimals).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]V/M: relationship prefixes with player IDs. '|ID' marks a master of this civ, and ',ID' marks a vassal of this civ (prefix style for compactness).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Trd: trade network connection with selected perspective.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]OB: open borders with selected perspective team.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]DP: defensive pact with selected perspective team.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Rel: state religion icon (holy city variant when applicable).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Dip: war/forced-peace state with selected perspective.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]WT: won't talk marker.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]WE: worst enemy marker.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]GA: golden age / anarchy marker.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Esp: active espionage spending weight against that team.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Tech: number of known techs for that player's team.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Res: current research icon only (no text).[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]%: current research progress percent (0-99).

[H1]World Advisor Territory Legend[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]The Territory tab counts plots and map assets owned by the selected player. It separates plots inside city big fat crosses from the rest of the player's owned land so empire-wide resources can be read without losing city-workable context.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]BFC: owned plots that are inside at least one selected-player city's big fat cross.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Sub: suburbs; owned plots outside all selected-player city big fat crosses. These plots are controlled territory, but no selected-player city can work them directly unless a city is founded, captured or culturally expanded differently later.[NEWLINE][ICON_BULLET]Tot: total owned plots or assets, equal to BFC plus Sub.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Some plot rows overlap because they describe different properties of the same plots. For example, a Peak is also Land, and a riverside Grassland is both River and Grassland. Therefore the Plots table's Total row is a real plot count, not the sum of every visible row.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]The World Advisor respects normal map knowledge: hidden or unexplored plots and bonuses stay hidden unless debug mode is active. More entries can therefore appear as territory and city BFCs become fully explored, or when debug mode lets you inspect another player.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Hover over a row icon for the usual tooltip, or click it to open the corresponding Sevopedia entry when one exists.

[H1]Sevopedia Leader Legend[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2][H2]Attitude / Action Buttons[\H2][PARAGRAPH:2]- Attitude buttons row (top): pick the leader mood preview shown in the 3D leaderhead. Emoji mode uses icons (furious, annoyed, cautious, pleased, friendly); text mode uses FU/AN/CA/PL/FR.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Action buttons row (bottom): trigger animation preview (no/idle reset, greeting, agree, disagree) for the selected leader.[PARAGRAPH:2][H2]Era Art Buttons[\H2][PARAGRAPH:2]- Only shown when the per-era leaderhead art SAS define is enabled and the leader has per-era art entries defined in XML.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Selected button shows an icon: lock icon = default art active; era icon = that era's art active. Unselected buttons show text: d = default, numbers = era index (0 = Ancient, 1 = Classical, 2 = Medieval, 3 = Renaissance, 4 = Industrial, 5 = Modern, 6 = Future in a standard 7-era setup; may differ if the mod uses a different era count or order). Only eras that have art defined appear. Clicking switches the leaderhead 3D preview to that era's art.[PARAGRAPH:2][H2]AI Personality Panel Legend[\H2][PARAGRAPH:2]- 1st column: "label", a shortened or full descriptive text, like/such as "T.W Likely" (a shortened explanation of what iMaxWarRand means ingame), as said earlier. This column contains the raw value too, for exhaustiveness, for example "T.W Likely (50)" for moctezuma, 50 is the raw value, or "T.W Likely (400)" for Gandhi, 400 is the raw value, for raw ai atributes. For aggregated ai aattributes, like contact probs, positive and negative memory resentments and affections, etc, the raw value is also displayed, but since there are many components (raw values) used to calculate the aggregated ai attribute, we instead display each of them "/" separated, for example for gandhi in the negative memory resentment category, the "D.W onFr (-200/120)" line shows that Gandhi has a raw ai attribute memory attitude percent of -200 (i.e. Gandhi resents quite strongly you declaring war on his friends) and a memory decay of 120. And for Moctezuma, for the same "D.W onFr" negative memory resentment, Moctezuma does not resent as much (memory attitude percent of -100, and memory decay same though of 120 (currently btw decays are same among all leaders (in civ4/advciv and thus advciv-sas too), not ideal but maybe functional))[PARAGRAPH:2]- 2nd column: the normalized score is displayed. As we explained before at the ranking calculation part, these scores are normalized, which means score is minimum 0 and maximum 100. Sometimes all leaders will have 50 (if same value among all leaders, happens sometimes in civ4 not ideal), and else leaders are ranked gradually from 0 (worse performer for better or worse) to 100 (best performer. Moctezuma performs good at war attributes, and gandhi good at peace attributes, for example). Note that this code is entirely dynamic, you can add more leaders and they'll get the new values and participate and possibly completely upset the best/worse performers ranking depending on their values (if they are different from current leaders ones, and how much), and you could also additionally tweak the existing leaders xml values. (If you think for example the roman leaders underperform in war, you may tweak their xml. As for aggregated ai attributes, they also display normalized scores, but since they are already normalized at their creation by definition, before leaders_data.py is (has finished being) generated), they are still displayed as normalized scores (min 0, max 100, unless all leaders have an equal score/value then all leaders get 50), but since they are already normalized, they are not re-normalized during caching and Sevopedia Leader computation. In short, they can be intepreted similarly as raw ai attributes.[PARAGRAPH:2]- (Inverted): when a line is marked (Inverted), lower raw values are better/more intense and higher raw values are weaker/less likely. Example: T.W Likely (getMaxWarRand) at 50 means much more war-prone than 400.[PARAGRAPH:2]- 3rd column: scale, this is an indicative display, raw ai attributes and aggregated ai attributes are graded graphically 10 by 10 with a symbol, but raw ai attributes use a "+" symbol, while ai aggregated symbols use a "#" symbol (trying to be visually clear while also civ4-compatible). So for example maxwarrand 400 gandhi has a score of 0 normalized among all leaders (it is highest score i.e Gandhi is the least war-like leader(s (if others have a value of 400 too))) so his scale is "++++++++++" (100th percentile), while moctezuma has a warrand of 50, score 100 among all leaders (very war-like (in a "T.W war Likely" way highest leader(s (if other have same value of 50)))), so his scale is "" (0th percentile). Aggregated ai attributes follow a similar logic, so for example for the contact offer probabilites ai aggregated attribute, shaka has a "Tr Tech (30/5)" score of 79, meanings he is likely, relatively to all leaders, to contact you for a tech trade offer, and 79th percentile is a bit short of 80 but still 7 scales/symbols so he will be "#######". If the value is 50 sharp, for raw ai attributes or ai aggregated attributes, the symbol will also be "+++++" or "#####". This is especially important because the last symbol is "=====", which only happens if, for both raw ai attributes and ai aggregated attributes, if all leaders have strictly the same raw value for this attribute aggregated or raw is same in this case functionally, i.e. they'll both get an equal scale symbol, and precisely 5 scales, also all leaders will scale 50. So 50 "+++++", 50 "#####", and 50 "=====" all have different meanings, one being respectively only for raw ai attributes scoring of said leader, the other of similarly same ai aggregated value of said leader, while the last being the display of an equal value among all leaders for an easier read.[PARAGRAPH:2][H2]Category Label -> Internal Key (Exhaustive, by category)[\H2][NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Core Personality:[NEWLINE]- Base Attitude -> getBaseAttitude.[NEWLINE]- Base Peace Weig -> getBasePeaceWeight.[NEWLINE]- Peace Weig Rand -> getPeaceWeightRand.[NEWLINE]- Worse Rank AC -> getWorseRankDifferenceAttitudeChange.[NEWLINE]- Better Rank AC -> getBetterRankDifferenceAttitudeChange.[NEWLINE]- Warmonger Resp -> getWarmongerRespect.[NEWLINE]- CloseBordSpark -> getCloseBordersAttitudeChange (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- Same Relig ACL -> getSameReligionAttitudeChangeLimit.[NEWLINE]- Diff Relig ACL -> getDifferentReligionAttitudeChangeLimit (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- Fav Civic ACL -> getFavoriteCivicAttitudeChangeLimit.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]BBAI Victory Weights:[NEWLINE]- Conquest -> getConquestVictoryWeight.[NEWLINE]- Domination -> getDominationVictoryWeight.[NEWLINE]- Culture -> getCultureVictoryWeight.[NEWLINE]- Diplomacy -> getDiplomacyVictoryWeight.[NEWLINE]- Space -> getSpaceVictoryWeight.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Flavors:[NEWLINE]- Military -> iFlavorMilitary.[NEWLINE]- Religion -> iFlavorReligion.[NEWLINE]- Production -> iFlavorProduction.[NEWLINE]- Gold -> iFlavorGold.[NEWLINE]- Science -> iFlavorScience.[NEWLINE]- Culture -> iFlavorCulture.[NEWLINE]- Growth -> iFlavorGrowth.[NEWLINE]- Espionage -> iFlavorEspionage.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]War Strategy:[NEWLINE]- T.W Likely -> getMaxWarRand (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- T.W NearPR -> getMaxWarNearbyPowerRatio.[NEWLINE]- T.W DistPR -> getMaxWarDistantPowerRatio.[NEWLINE]- T.W MinNearPR -> getMaxWarMinAdjacentLandPercent (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- Lim.W Likely -> getLimitedWarRand (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- Lim.W PR -> getLimitedWarPowerRatio.[NEWLINE]- Risky Aggr -> getBaseAttackOddsChange.[NEWLINE]- Risky AggrRand+ -> getAttackOddsChangeRand.[NEWLINE]- Raz C % -> getRazeCityProb.[NEWLINE]- TribRSneakW% -> getDemandRebukedSneakProb.[NEWLINE]- TribRef W% -> getDemandRebukedWarProb.[NEWLINE]- Dogpile Likely -> getDogpileWarRand (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- WAllianceMaker -> getDeclareWarTradeRand.[NEWLINE]- Share W ACL -> getShareWarAttitudeChangeLimit.[NEWLINE]- ResistCapitulPM -> getVassalPowerModifier.[NEWLINE]- Ref2TalkWSpan -> getRefuseToTalkWarThreshold.[NEWLINE]- MakePeaceLikely -> getMakePeaceRand (Inverted).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Economic Preferences:[NEWLINE]- Max Gold Tr% -> getMaxGoldTradePercent.[NEWLINE]- Max GPT Tr% -> getMaxGoldPerTurnTradePercent.[NEWLINE]- NoTechYetRdy% -> getTechTradeKnownPercent.[NEWLINE]- NoTech2AdvT -> getNoTechTradeThreshold (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- Build Unit % -> getBuildUnitProb.[NEWLINE]- Wonder C.R -> getWonderConstructRand.[NEWLINE]- EspionageWeig -> getEspionageWeight.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Contact Offer Probabilities:[NEWLINE]- PeaceTreaty -> iAggregatedContactProbPeaceTreaty.[NEWLINE]- Open Borders -> iAggregatedContactProbOpenBorders.[NEWLINE]- Tr Map -> iAggregatedContactProbTradeMap.[NEWLINE]- Tr Tech -> iAggregatedContactProbTradeTech.[NEWLINE]- Tr Bonus -> iAggregatedContactProbTradeBonus.[NEWLINE]- Gave Help -> iAggregatedContactProbGiveHelp.[NEWLINE]- DefensPact -> iAggregatedContactProbDefensivePact.[NEWLINE]- PermAlliance -> iAggregatedContactProbPermanentAlliance.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Contact Demand Probabilities:[NEWLINE]- Relig Press -> iAggregatedContactProbReligionPressure.[NEWLINE]- Civic Press -> iAggregatedContactProbCivicPressure.[NEWLINE]- Stop Tr -> iAggregatedContactProbStopTrading.[NEWLINE]- Trib -> iAggregatedContactProbDemandTribute.[NEWLINE]- Help -> iAggregatedContactProbAskForHelp.[NEWLINE]- Join W -> iAggregatedContactProbJoinWar.[NEWLINE]- Contact probabilities raw pair shown in label: getContactRand(iContactType)/getContactDelay(iContactType).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Refusal Thresholds (Offer):[NEWLINE]- Open Bord -> getOpenBordersRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- Map -> getMapRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- Tech -> getTechRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- Strategic Bonus -> getStrategicBonusRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- Happiness Bonus -> getHappinessBonusRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- Health Bonus -> getHealthBonusRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- PrideNoHelp -> getNoGiveHelpAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- DefensPact -> getDefensivePactRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Refusal Thresholds (Demand):[NEWLINE]- Convert Religion -> getConvertReligionRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- Adopt Civic -> getAdoptCivicRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- D.W -> getDeclareWarRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- LoyaltyNoD.W -> getDeclareWarThemRefuseAttitudeThreshold (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- StopTr -> getStopTradingRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- LoyaltyNoStopTr -> getStopTradingThemRefuseAttitudeThreshold (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- ScaryNoTrib -> getDemandTributeAttitudeThreshold (Inverted).[NEWLINE]- C -> getCityRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- Native C -> getNativeCityRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE]- Vassal -> getVassalRefuseAttitudeThreshold.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]No War At:[NEWLINE]- Furious -> iNoWarAttitudeProbFurious.[NEWLINE]- Annoyed -> iNoWarAttitudeProbAnnoyed.[NEWLINE]- Cautious -> iNoWarAttitudeProbCautious.[NEWLINE]- Pleased -> iNoWarAttitudeProbPleased.[NEWLINE]- Friendly -> iNoWarAttitudeProbFriendly.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Attitude Changes:[NEWLINE]- Same Relig AC -> getSameReligionAttitudeChange.[NEWLINE]- Same Relig AD -> getSameReligionAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- Diff Relig AC -> getDifferentReligionAttitudeChange.[NEWLINE]- Diff Relig AD -> getDifferentReligionAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- Fav Civic AC -> getFavoriteCivicAttitudeChange.[NEWLINE]- Fav Civic AD -> getFavoriteCivicAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- Lost W AC -> getLostWarAttitudeChange.[NEWLINE]- At W AD -> getAtWarAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- At W ACL -> getAtWarAttitudeChangeLimit.[NEWLINE]- At Peace AD -> getAtPeaceAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- At Peace ACL -> getAtPeaceAttitudeChangeLimit.[NEWLINE]- Share W AC -> getShareWarAttitudeChange.[NEWLINE]- Share W AD -> getShareWarAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- Bonus Tr AD -> getBonusTradeAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- Bonus Tr ACL -> getBonusTradeAttitudeChangeLimit.[NEWLINE]- Open Bord AD -> getOpenBordersAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- Open Bord ACL -> getOpenBordersAttitudeChangeLimit.[NEWLINE]- Defens Pact AD -> getDefensivePactAttitudeDivisor.[NEWLINE]- Defens Pact ACL -> getDefensivePactAttitudeChangeLimit.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Misc Modifiers:[NEWLINE]- FreedomApprec -> getFreedomAppreciation.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Positive Memory Affections:[NEWLINE]- Gave Help -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryGiveHelpAffection.[NEWLINE]- AcD -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryAcceptDemandAffection.[NEWLINE]- AcRelig -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryAcceptedReligionAffection.[NEWLINE]- AcCivic -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryAcceptedCivicAffection.[NEWLINE]- AcJoin W -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryAcceptedJoinWarAffection.[NEWLINE]- AcStop Tr -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryAcceptedStopTradingAffection.[NEWLINE]- VotedForUs -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryVotedForUsAffection.[NEWLINE]- Event Good -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryEventGoodToUsAffection.[NEWLINE]- LiberatedC -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryLiberatedCitiesAffection.[NEWLINE]- Indep -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryIndependenceAffection.[NEWLINE]- Tr Tech -> iAggregatedPositiveMemoryTradedTechToUsAffection.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE]Negative Memory Resentments:[NEWLINE]- D.W -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryDeclaredWarResentment.[NEWLINE]- D.W onFr -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryDeclaredWarOnFriendResentment.[NEWLINE]- HirWAlly -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryHiredWarAllyResentment.[NEWLINE]- Nuked Us -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryNukedUsResentment.[NEWLINE]- Nuked Fr -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryNukedFriendResentment.[NEWLINE]- RazC -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryRazedCityResentment.[NEWLINE]- RazHolyC -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryRazedHolyCityResentment.[NEWLINE]- Spy Caught -> iAggregatedNegativeMemorySpyCaughtResentment.[NEWLINE]- RefHelpUs -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryRefusedHelpResentment.[NEWLINE]- Rej D -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryRejectedDemandResentment.[NEWLINE]- Dn Relig -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryDeniedReligionResentment.[NEWLINE]- Dn Civic -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryDeniedCivicResentment.[NEWLINE]- Dn JoinW -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryDeniedJoinWarResentment.[NEWLINE]- Dn StopTr -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryDeniedStopTradingResentment.[NEWLINE]- StoppedTr -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryStoppedTradingResentment.[NEWLINE]- TrEmbargo -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryHiredTradeEmbargoResentment.[NEWLINE]- Made D -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryMadeDemandResentment.[NEWLINE]- VotedAgUs -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryVotedAgainstUsResentment.[NEWLINE]- Event Bad -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryEventBadToUsResentment.[NEWLINE]- CancVassal -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryCancelledVassalAgreementResentment.[NEWLINE]- Recent W -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryDeclaredWarRecentResentment.[NEWLINE]- RecentTechAny -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryReceivedTechFromAnyResentment.[NEWLINE]- RecentMadeD -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryMadeDemandRecentResentment.[NEWLINE]- CancelledOB -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryCancelledOpenBordersResentment.[NEWLINE]- CancelledDP -> iAggregatedNegativeMemoryCancelledDefensivePactResentment.[NEWLINE]- Memory raw pair shown in label: getMemoryAttitudePercent(iMemoryType)/getMemoryDecayRand(iMemoryType).[PARAGRAPH:2]See docs for details about the AI Personality Panel, notably _1_AdvCiv-SAS\Docs\README_AI_Personality_Panel.md.

[H1]Sevopedia numTxt Legend[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]This page explains abbreviated labels shown under icon buttons in Sevopedia Building, Unit, Terrain, and Feature pages.[PARAGRAPH:2][H2]Building page numTxt[\H2][PARAGRAPH:2]- InC: required in city (the prerequisite building must be in the same city).[PARAGRAPH:2]- R: world-size requirement modifier (not random). This is the same R used inside AC N+R.[PARAGRAPH:2]- AC N+R: required in all cities. N = base XML requirement, R = world-size requirement modifier from World Size iBuildingClassPrereqModifier (rounded up). [PARAGRAPH:2]- Example for AC with N=2: Duel/Tiny (modifier 0) => 2; Standard (+38) => 3; Huge (+75) => 4.[PARAGRAPH:2]- ACs: applies to all cities.[PARAGRAPH:2]- AUC: applies to all eligible unit classes/combats.[PARAGRAPH:2]- (!)Cvc: this prerequisite can be bypassed or altered by civic rules.[PARAGRAPH:2]- OR: alternative prerequisite grouping; one of the OR-linked icons can satisfy that part.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Plain number (for example 1, 2, 3): exact count linked to that icon (for example free specialists count).[PARAGRAPH:2]- FBBR (when shown alone for free bonus count): world-size dependent free bonus count (iNumFreeBuildingBonuses). In this mod, for example: Standard=4, Huge=6.[PARAGRAPH:2][H2]Unit/Terrain/Feature page numTxt[\H2][PARAGRAPH:2]- +A/+D: attack and defense percent modifiers.[PARAGRAPH:2]- +A/_: attack-only modifier.[PARAGRAPH:2]- _/+D: defense-only modifier.[PARAGRAPH:2]- _/_: no attack/defense modifier.[PARAGRAPH:2]- +X%: single percent modifier (for example combat-class modifier).[PARAGRAPH:2]- N: unit has free Navigator promotion.[PARAGRAPH:2]- HM1, HM2, HM3: unit has free Hills Master tier 1/2/3. Contiguous tiers use ranges (for example HM1-3); gaps keep addition (for example HM1+3).[PARAGRAPH:2]- W1, W2, W3: unit has free Woodsman tier 1/2/3. Combined forms (for example W1+2+3) indicate multiple free tiers.

[H1]Sevopedia Categories Legend[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]This page explains shortened category labels used in the left Sevopedia category list.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Corporat.: Corporations.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Event Tri.: Event Triggers.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Improv.: Improvements.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Mods Info: custom AdvCiv-SAS concept pages and legends.[PARAGRAPH:2]- N. Wond.: National Wonders.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Outdated: legacy/reference pages kept for compatibility/history.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Pr. List: Promotion List.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Pr. Tree: Promotion Tree.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Un. Chart: Unit Chart[PARAGRAPH:2]- Un. Tree: Unit Upgrade Tree.[PARAGRAPH:2]- W. Sizes: World Sizes.[PARAGRAPH:2]- W. Wond.: World Wonders.[PARAGRAPH:2]Labels are shortened mainly to fit sidebar width cleanly at different resolutions and UI scales.

[H1]Sevopedia Speed Chart Legend[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]This page explains abbreviated labels used in the Sevopedia Game Speed chart.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Inc Y XX: increments years chunk XX.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Inc M XX: increments months chunk XX.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Cal XX: calendar row XX.[PARAGRAPH:2]- S XX (N%): summary row XX at N percent of total turns.

[H1]Sevopedia Votes Legend[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]This page explains abbreviations used in Sevopedia Votes.[PARAGRAPH:2]List abbreviations:[PARAGRAPH:2]- Source prefixes are removed in entries because votes are already grouped by source section headers (for example, United Nations and Apostolic Palace).[PARAGRAPH:2]- E: means Election.[PARAGRAPH:2]- R# means Resolution #.[PARAGRAPH:2]Panel abbreviations:[PARAGRAPH:2]- V.Source means Vote Source.[PARAGRAPH:2]- S.Civic means Vote Source Civic.[PARAGRAPH:2]- S.Free.Sp means Vote Source Free Specialist.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Fo.Civics means Forced Civics.[PARAGRAPH:2]Scope difference:[PARAGRAPH:2]- Vote Source fields (V.Source, S.Civic, S.Free.Sp, Vote Source Info) apply to the vote source itself and are shared by votes that belong to that source.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Vote fields (Requirements, Effects, Fo.Civics) apply to the selected vote/resolution entry.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Full vote names remain visible in the Name panel.

[H1]Sevopedia Event Triggers Legend[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]This page explains the era groupings and abbreviations used in Sevopedia Event Triggers.[PARAGRAPH:2]Era grouping:[PARAGRAPH:2]- Any era (no tech requirement): triggers that can fire from turn 1 because they declare no tech prerequisite of any kind.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Ancient / Classical / Medieval / ... : triggers whose earliest tech prerequisite is visible directly on the trigger XML, i.e. in its OrPreReqs or AndPreReqs tech list. These are "direct" era classifications - the gate is explicit on the trigger itself.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Ancient (indirect) / Classical (indirect) / ... : triggers whose era was inferred via chain lookup because the trigger XML does not list a direct tech prereq. The era comes from one of: OtherPlayerHasTech (another player must have a tech), a prerequisite event trigger's inferred era, the required Civic's prereq tech, a required Building/Unit class's default building/unit prereq tech, a required Religion's prereq tech, or a required Corporation's prereq tech. These are "indirect" - the trigger never reads "requires Flight" directly, but it requires something that implies Flight.[PARAGRAPH:2]Random event selection:[PARAGRAPH:2]- iWeight and Active in Games affect the normal automatic random-event lottery. Manual or scripted triggering can still force an event trigger outside that lottery, so iWeight 0 or Active in Games 0% does not necessarily mean the XML entry is unused.[PARAGRAPH:2]Requirements display:[PARAGRAPH:2]- Requires (Buttons) shows linkable requirements such as techs, civics, buildings, units, religions, corporations, terrains, features, improvements, bonuses, and prerequisite event triggers.[PARAGRAPH:2]- Requires (Text) shows numeric, flag, route, target-selection, and Python-gate constraints that have no clear pedia button target.[PARAGRAPH:2]Numbered labels (numTxt) under buttons:[PARAGRAPH:2]- Oth.P. : other player has this tech. Pairs with the OtherPlayerHasTech field. This tech does not need to be researched by the trigger's player - someone else in the game must have it. The label appears because the icon art is identical to a self-prereq tech icon and would otherwise be ambiguous.[PARAGRAPH:2]- OR : appears between adjacent buttons in a Requires OR-prereq group of two or more techs, between same-field plot alternatives such as terrain/feature/improvement/bonus options, or between adjacent Obsolete with techs when multiple techs can obsolete the trigger. Means any single one satisfies that group; for Obsolete with, any single one blocks the trigger.[PARAGRAPH:2]- No connector between adjacent buttons means AND: all shown requirements apply. AND is left implicit because writing "and" between every required icon makes dense panels harder to scan.
